author: Dr. Rachel Imani (Hepatology)
content:
HEPATOLOGY CLINIC NOTE — UPMC
Date: 4/10/2027

SUBJECTIVE: Referred for ~3 months of unintentional weight loss (down from ~67 kg to ~61 kg) with poor appetite, on a background of alcohol-related cirrhosis with ongoing alcohol use. Increasing abdominal girth recently.

EVALUATION OF INVOLUNTARY WEIGHT LOSS (initiated):
- History/exam: cachexia, ongoing alcohol use, poor intake; screen for GI symptoms, no overt GI bleeding reported at this visit.
- Labs (LAB_HX_001): worsening synthetic markers; mild anemia; normal TSH and HbA1c; AFP mildly elevated (18, nonspecific in cirrhosis).
- Differential considered: progression of cirrhosis with anorexia/malnutrition; MALIGNANCY (hepatocellular carcinoma given cirrhosis; other given smoking and family history of breast cancer); malabsorption; depression/social factors limiting intake.
- Plan: arrange dedicated multiphase liver imaging for HCC surveillance; nutrition referral; age-appropriate cancer screening; reinforce alcohol cessation; close follow-up.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Involuntary weight loss — workup as above; imaging pending.
#Decompensated cirrhosis — continue lactulose/rifaximin; monitor for further decompensation.
#Alcohol use disorder — counseling; resources offered.
(Note: she presented acutely with a variceal bleed before the planned imaging was completed.)
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Rachel Imani, MD (Hepatology). 4/10/2027.
